expectations.text: Recognizes --team flag and sets mode to team
expectations.evidence: Section 1 'Argument Parsing and Mode Detection' shows explicit parsing: '--team flag is not a named argument in the frontmatter — it appears inline in the feature_description string.' Result: 'mode = "team"', 'USE_TEAM_MODE = true'.

expectations.text: Passes --team flag through to spec-plan invocation
expectations.evidence: Section 3 (Step 1) shows: 'Skill: spec-plan, Args: "Build a complete notification system with real-time WebSocket events, email digests, and in-app notification center --team"'. The --team flag is explicitly re-appended per SKILL.md Step 1 instructions.

Section 7 (Step 5) states: 'Because mode == "team", the orchestrator routes to start-phase-execute-team, NOT start-phase-execute.' Invocation shows 'Skill: start-phase-execute-team'. Summary table (Section 8) confirms routing.

expectations.text: Still follows the 5-step sequence even in team mode
expectations.evidence: Sections 3-7 maintain exact order: spec-plan (1) → spec-review (2) → start-phase-plan (3) → pm-db (4) → start-phase-execute-team (5). Section 12 explicitly states: 'Team mode does not reorder or skip steps.'

eval_feedback.overall: All assertions pass with detailed evidence. Team mode routing is clearly documented.
